description: Hark llamadart quantization benchmark — full Q8/Q4 matrix across Qwen3 0.6B and Qwen2.5 0.5B Instruct, plus EmbeddingGemma 300M Q8_0. Trust tier is first-party only: ggml-org for EmbeddingGemma + Qwen3 (0.6B Q4_0 since Q4_K_M is not published by any first-party source for this model), Qwen team for Qwen2.5 (full quant ladder available, we test Q8_0 and Q4_K_M). The bench measures all quants for every model regardless of pass/fail so we get the full latency curve in one run — see escalation_policy.
escalation_policy_note: When set to 'measure_all_quants', the bench runs every quant for every model and records its perf and quality regardless of whether the gate passes. When set to 'q8-only' (the original behavior), the runner stops at the first quant that passes the quality gate. We use 'measure_all_quants' for exploration phases where we need the full latency curve to make architectural decisions.
trust_tier_note: Current main branch ships onnx-community/embeddinggemma-300m-ONNX (model_q4.onnx) and litert-community/Qwen3-0.6B (Qwen3-0.6B.litertlm). These are the format-specific first-party orgs for ONNX and LiteRT-LM respectively. ggml-org and Qwen are the equivalent first-party tier for GGUF.

quants.publisher_note: ggml-org is the official llama.cpp team — the GGUF format's inventors and the GGUF equivalent of onnx-community for ONNX.

ggml-org publishes Qwen3-0.6B in Q4_0, Q8_0, f16. Q4_K_M is NOT published by any first-party source for Qwen3-0.6B (neither ggml-org nor Qwen team), so Q4_0 is the only first-party Q4 option. Q4_0 is the older block-wise quantization (32 weights per block, single FP16 scale), historically slightly worse quality than Q4_K_M but a valid first-party choice.
Published by the Qwen team themselves — directly from Alibaba's model authors, same source as the litert-community Qwen3-0.6B.litertlm we currently ship.

Qwen team publishes the full quant ladder (Q2_K, Q3_K_M, Q4_0, Q4_K_M, Q5_0, Q5_K_M, Q6_K, Q8_0, fp16) for Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct. WARNING: Qwen2.5-0.5B is BIGGER on disk than Qwen3-0.6B at every quant level because the 151k-token vocab embedding table dominates GGUF file size at this parameter scale. The '0.5B' name refers to transformer body params, not total file size. Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct Q4_K_M is 480 MB; Qwen3-0.6B-Q4_0 is 409 MB (smaller). We test it anyway because the architecture and chat template differ.

Same first-party Qwen repo, Q8_0 quant. Plain ChatML chat template — no thinking-mode complexity. ~657 MB on disk (LARGER than Qwen3-0.6B Q8_0 at 581 MB) due to vocab embedding overhead — see Q4_K_M note.

embedder_only_migration.when: If no Qwen variant in this matrix passes both quality and latency gates on the target hardware (Moto G56 5G CPU), abandon the on-device generative slot-filling migration. Keep llamadart for embeddings (clear win), and either (a) escalate slot-filling to a cloud LLM, (b) replace it with an encoder-based slot tagger (Track 2 in docs/plans/llamadart-migration-findings.md), or (c) keep flutter_gemma as a no-op fallback while Track 2 ships.
embedder_only_migration.implication: The migration still ships for the embedder. Slot-filling becomes a separate workstream with its own architecture decision.

community_quant_escalation.when: If we later need a smaller quant than Q4_0 / Q4_K_M for file-size or latency reasons, the fallback is a trusted community quantizer (unsloth, bartowski, mradermacher) — but this is an EXPLICIT trust decision, not a default. Any switch away from ggml-org / Qwen must be justified by measured size or perf wins that cannot be ignored.
